contents:
# 50 suspicious Russian vessels map Baltic undersea cables via anomalous routes

## Overview
At least 50 suspicious vessels identified following anomalous navigation routes over communication cables and wind farms in the Baltic. These vessels use flag hopping, stolen IMO numbers from zombie ships, and GNSS spoofing to manipulate identities. Network evolved from Russian oil price cap evasion to surveillance and potential sabotage of critical infrastructure.

## Details
Tactics include spoofing GNSS signals for false locations on maritime systems and ship-to-ship transfers with clean vessels. Applies to oil tankers and covert ops vessels monitoring energy infrastructure, undersea power/comms cables, and testing naval forces. Aim remains creating target library for armed conflict or grey zone operations to paralyze Europe. Significant increase in practices detected in Gulf of Oman and Baltic for sanctioned tankers.

**Location**: Baltic Sea

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @TheRealShipDude, which covers ship identification, sanctions evasion vessels, AIS analysis. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following ship identification will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
